title: Replace 100vh with 100dvh in chat layout
description: In src/features/chat/ChatPage.tsx and src/features/chat/LLMChat.tsx, replace 'calc(100vh - 64px)' and other 100vh usages with 100dvh so mobile browsers don't clip behind the URL bar. Verify the header offset still matches: if mobile header height differs, use { xs: 'calc(100dvh - <mobile>px)', md: 'calc(100vh - 64px)' }. This is purely additive on desktop (dvh == vh when no dynamic UI).

title: AdminPage tables → card list on xs
description: In src/features/admin/AdminPage.tsx, the Sessions (6 cols) and Feedback (4 cols) hand-rolled HTML tables are unusable on phones. Below sm, render each row as an MUI List item / Card with primary title + preview + secondary metadata stacked. Keep table view at sm+. Rows remain click-to-open-dialog. Header on the cards can be tighter than the table headers.

title: AdminPage responsive filter row
description: In src/features/admin/AdminPage.tsx, the filter row has 4 TextFields (200/160/160/200 fixed widths) + Search/Clear buttons. On xs: each TextField becomes width: '100%' (or { xs: '100%', sm: 200 } per field), Search/Clear buttons become fullWidth. Use existing flexWrap: 'wrap' container. Also: analytics chart parent height becomes { xs: 200, md: 250 }.

title: fullScreen dialogs on xs viewports
description: Add fullScreen={isXs} (via useMediaQuery(theme.breakpoints.down('sm'))) to: src/features/chat/DatasetsDialog.tsx, src/features/admin/AdminPage.tsx session detail dialog, and the feedback dialog in AdminPage.tsx. Also check AboutDialog and FeedbackDialog in src/features/chat/ — apply fullScreen on xs if their content benefits from it.

title: SchemaDrawer master/detail navigation on xs
description: In src/features/chat/SchemaDrawer.tsx, the internal layout is a fixed 220px rail + flex content. PaperProps already use width: { xs: '100%', sm: 720 }. On xs (< sm), refactor to single-pane master/detail: show the list (rail content full width); clicking an item navigates to the detail view with a back button header. On sm+, keep side-by-side layout. Use local React state for selected item navigation (or build on existing schema hash route).

title: MessageRating icons always visible on touch
description: In src/features/chat/MessageRating.tsx (and its callsite in LLMChat.tsx), the icons use opacity: 0 unless hoveredMessageId === message.id. On mobile this means ratings are invisible. Make icons always visible below md (theme.breakpoints.down('md')) by passing a forceVisible prop or reading the breakpoint locally. Keep desktop hover behavior. Already-rated messages should already show icon — verify.

title: Responsive LLMChat Options row + input paddings
description: In src/features/chat/LLMChat.tsx, the 'Options' collapse contains two RadioGroup row controls with a borderLeft divider. On xs: change inner flexDirection to column, remove borderLeft (use borderTop or none). Reduce input Paper padding on xs (p: { xs: 1, md: 2 }). Keep flexWrap behavior on sm+.

title: Always-visible delete in ChatHistorySidebar on touch
description: In src/features/chat/ChatHistorySidebar.tsx, drop the hover gate on the delete icon below md. Use useMediaQuery(theme.breakpoints.down('md')) so desktop keeps hover-reveal but touch/mobile sees the icon always. Consider using ListItemButton secondaryAction pattern. Optionally accept an onSelect callback that also triggers parent drawer close.

title: Overflow Menu for ChatPage action buttons on xs
description: In src/features/chat/ChatPage.tsx, collapse the secondary action buttons (About, Feedback, MCP/API Keys, Datasets, Tables, Export) into an overflow IconButton + Menu on < md. Keep Share/Fork (and New Chat if applicable) inline. Implement as an actions array + conditional render so the same source defines both desktop row and mobile menu items.

title: Mobile drawer for ChatHistorySidebar
description: In src/features/chat/ChatPage.tsx, replace the fixed 280px sidebar Box with conditional render: permanent Box on >= md, MUI Drawer anchor='left' variant='temporary' on < md. Add mobileDrawerOpen state and a hamburger IconButton in the header. Pass an onClose prop to ChatHistorySidebar so selecting a session auto-closes the drawer on mobile. Remove the 280px header spacer below md.

title: Responsive ChatPage header layout
description: In src/features/chat/ChatPage.tsx, add useMediaQuery(theme.breakpoints.down('md')) and make the header stack on xs. Apply responsive sx: flexDirection: { xs: 'column', md: 'row' }, smaller logo (height: { xs: 36, md: 60 }), title variant: { xs: 'h6', md: 'h5' }, smaller gap on xs. Keep desktop layout identical. This establishes the breakpoint pattern reused by sibling tasks.

title: Feature: Make chat and admin pages mobile-responsive
description: The chat page (src/features/chat/) and admin page (src/features/admin/) currently work on desktop only. Make them functional and usable on mobile devices (phones, small tablets). Constraints: minimize impact on desktop UX; small desktop UX changes are acceptable if they make the code cleaner. Key files: ChatPage.tsx (786 lines), LLMChat.tsx (1234 lines), ChatHistorySidebar.tsx, SchemaDrawer.tsx, AdminPage.tsx (711 lines). Goal: full mobile functionality without device-specific code forks where possible.

title: Auto-linkify SQL view names in chat markdown
description:
Goal: when the assistant mentions a view name like 'colocalization_v' or 'credible_sets_v' in chat text, render it as a clickable link that opens the SchemaDrawer at that view via the #schema/<view> hash.

Scope:
- Edit src/features/chat/LLMChat.tsx (ReactMarkdown rendering around line ~391).
- Add a rehype plugin (or remark plugin) that walks text nodes and replaces matches of /\b(credible_sets_v|colocalization_v|coloc_credsets_v|exome_variant_results_v|gene_burden_results_v)(\.[a-z0-9_]+)?\b/ with an <a href='#schema/$1'> link. The optional .column part is shown but the link goes to the view (column highlight can come later).
- IMPORTANT: skip text inside fenced code blocks and inline code (rehype walking should naturally skip <code> ancestors; verify).
- Pull the list of view names from useSchema() instead of hardcoding once mj1.2 is wired — fallback to the 5 known names if schema is still loading.
- No prompt change to the LLM — purely client-side.

Files:
- src/features/chat/LLMChat.tsx (add plugin to ReactMarkdown rehypePlugins / remarkPlugins)
- src/features/chat/linkifyViews.ts (new — the plugin itself)

Test: in dev, send a message that mentions 'colocalization_v' and verify clicking opens the drawer at the right view; verify a code block containing 'colocalization_v' is NOT linkified.

title: Add hash-based deep linking for SchemaDrawer (#schema/<view>)
description:
Goal: opening URL '#schema/colocalization_v' opens the drawer with that view selected. Selecting a view in the drawer updates the hash. Closing the drawer clears it.

Scope:
- In ChatPage.tsx (or a small custom hook useSchemaHashRoute):
  - On mount and on hashchange, parse window.location.hash. If it matches /^#schema\/([a-z0-9_]+)$/, set schemaDrawerOpen=true and selectedView=match[1].
  - When the user selects a view in the drawer, push '#schema/<name>' via history.replaceState (avoid spamming the back stack).
  - On drawer close, clear the hash (replaceState to '').
- Guard: the hash routing only triggers for known view names (cross-check against useSchema().tables).
- Sharing a link with the hash to another user should land them on chat with the drawer open on the right view.

Files:
- src/features/chat/ChatPage.tsx (or src/features/chat/useSchemaHashRoute.ts)

title: Wire SchemaDrawer into ChatPage header
description:
Goal: add a 'Tables' header button that opens SchemaDrawer, mirroring the existing dialog pattern.

Scope:
- Modify src/features/chat/ChatPage.tsx:
  - Add state: const [schemaDrawerOpen, setSchemaDrawerOpen] = useState(false); selectedView state initialized to first view.
  - Add a header button placed next to the existing 'Datasets' button (existing dialog buttons are around lines 611–623).
  - Use an icon consistent with the rest (e.g., TableViewIcon from @mui/icons-material).
  - Render <SchemaDrawer> alongside the existing dialogs (around line 740–743).
- Do NOT remove or restyle the existing 'Datasets' button — they remain separate concerns (datasets = high-level catalog from /api/v1/datasets; tables = SQL views from /schema).

Files:
- src/features/chat/ChatPage.tsx (modify)

Out of scope: hash routing (mj1.5), markdown auto-linkify (mj1.6).

title: Build SchemaDrawer component (presentational)
description:
Goal: a self-contained MUI right-anchored Drawer that displays the schema catalog. Pure presentation — receives selectedView + onSelect via props.

Scope:
- New file: src/features/chat/SchemaDrawer.tsx
- MUI Drawer: anchor='right', variant='temporary', width ~480px.
- Left rail: List of the 5 views (credible_sets_v, colocalization_v, coloc_credsets_v, exome_variant_results_v, gene_burden_results_v) with row counts as secondary text.
- Right pane (or stacked when narrow):
  - View name + description
  - Columns table: name | type | mode | description | allowed_values (chips) | examples (mono font)
  - Example queries: collapsible Accordion list of {title, sql} with a Copy button
- Loading skeleton while useSchema is fetching, error state with retry.
- Reads via the useSchema hook from mj1.2.
- No header button wiring yet — that comes in mj1.4.
- No URL/hash logic — that comes in mj1.5.

Props (initial):
  { open: boolean; onClose: () => void; selectedView: string | null; onSelectView: (name: string) => void }

Files:
- src/features/chat/SchemaDrawer.tsx (new)
- (optional) small CSS-in-JS / sx styles inline

title: Add useSchema React Query hook + TypeScript types
description:
Goal: typed data layer for /schema, cached via React Query so SchemaDrawer mounts fast.

Scope:
- Add TS interfaces matching the SchemaResponse shape returned by genetics-results-db /schema:
  - resources: array of {name, label, description, aliases?}
  - tables: array of {name, description, row_count, columns: [{name, type, mode, description, allowed_values?, examples?}], examples: [{title, sql}], collection_resources?}
- Add a useSchema() hook using @tanstack/react-query (existing in this repo — see src/api/serverQuery.ts pattern).
- Stale time generous (schema rarely changes; ~10 min ok). Single fetch shared across the app.

Files to add/modify:
- src/features/chat/useSchema.ts (or src/api/schemaQuery.ts — follow existing pattern in src/api/)
- src/types/schema.ts (or inline in the hook file if small)

No UI changes in this subtask.

title: Verify /schema endpoint reachability from browser origin
description:
Goal: confirm what URL the React app should hit for GET /schema and add a proxy if needed. No UI work.

Investigate:
- genetics-results-db serves GET /schema at api/main.py ~line 282. Determine its public origin in dev and prod (env var likely VITE_API_URL or similar — check .env.dev / .env.prod in genetics-results-browser).
- Check CORS config in genetics-results-db (api/main.py CORS middleware). Add browser origins to allowlist if missing.
- Alternative: if cleaner, proxy /schema through the existing chat backend gateway (genetics-mcp-server chat_api.py) and consume from there.

Deliverable:
- A decided fetch URL (env-driven) documented in the issue close-comment.
- CORS or proxy changes committed if needed.
- A quick `curl` against dev URL confirming JSON response.

Files likely touched (if any):
- genetics-results-db/api/main.py (CORS allowlist)
- OR genetics-mcp-server/src/genetics_mcp_server/chat_api.py (new /schema proxy)

comments.author: juhis
comments.text:
Investigation complete. Chose Option (b): proxy /schema through genetics-mcp-server chat_api as GET /chat/v1/schema.

Rationale: browser today does NOT talk to genetics-results-db directly. Today's frontend topology:
- VITE_API_URL -> genetics-results-api FastAPI (/api/v1/*: datasets, search, credible_sets, ...)
- VITE_CHAT_URL -> genetics-mcp-server chat_api (/chat/v1/*: chat, sessions, tools, tokens, ...)

genetics-results-db (the new BigQuery service serving /schema, /query, /stats) is a SEPARATE Cloud Run service reached only server-to-server by the MCP executor via BIGQUERY_API_URL. Option (a) would require either a new VITE_BIGQUERY_URL env var (frontend + deploy work) or adding a new /schema route in the ingress — both are more invasive than option (b). CORS isn't the issue (genetics-results-db already has allow_origins=["*"]) — the issue is the URL isn't exposed to the browser and adding a new env var/ingress is more work than a tiny proxy.

Option (b) wins because: reuses existing VITE_CHAT_URL, existing httpx client in the executor, existing BIGQUERY_API_URL, existing CORS config on chat_api, existing auth_required dependency. Zero new env vars, zero ingress changes, zero frontend env changes.

Files modified:
- genetics-mcp-server/src/genetics_mcp_server/chat_api.py — added @app.get("/chat/v1/schema") that delegates to get_llm_service().executor.get_bigquery_schema(table=...). Returns the SchemaResponse JSON directly (unwrapping the executor's {"success": True, "schema": ...} envelope), with 502 on upstream errors and 503 if executor not initialized. Optional ?table=<view_name> query param is passed through.

Frontend URL the React app should fetch (subtask mj1.2):
- `${import.meta.env.VITE_CHAT_URL}/v1/schema` — i.e. /chat/v1/schema in prod, http://localhost:4000/chat/v1/schema in dev — same pattern as src/features/chat/chatHistoryApi.ts and tokenApi.ts.

Verification:
- Curl against the underlying db service confirmed the schema endpoint works locally:
  curl -s http://localhost:8080/schema  -> HTTP 200, JSON starting with `{"resources":{"finngen":...`
- The chat_api itself was not running on this machine, so the proxy was not curl-verified end-to-end. User should run, after starting chat_api locally (uvicorn genetics_mcp_server.chat_api:app --port 8000):
  curl -s http://localhost:8000/chat/v1/schema | jq '.tables[].name'
  Expected: list of view names (credible_sets_v, colocalization_v, coloc_credsets_v, exome_variant_results_v, gene_burden_results_v).

No frontend changes were made — useSchema hook and UI come in later subtasks mj1.2+.

title: Feature: Show available SQL views with READMEs in chat UI
description:
User feedback: 'it's hard to double check what the agent is doing since I don't know e.g. what colocalization_v is, and some of the column names I also can't guess. Maybe it would be helpful to give the user the list of tables with READMEs.'

Goal: Expose the SQL view catalog (5 views: credible_sets_v, colocalization_v, coloc_credsets_v, exome_variant_results_v, gene_burden_results_v) with descriptions, column lists, allowed values, and example queries to the chat user so they can sanity-check what the agent is doing.

Key context from exploration:
- Browser (genetics-results-browser): chat UI is src/features/chat/ChatPage.tsx. Header already has 4 dialogs: AboutDialog, FeedbackDialog, DatasetsDialog (uses /api/v1/datasets — dataset-level not view-level), McpTokenDialog. State managed by React + React Query.
- DB API (genetics-results-db/api/main.py): GET /schema endpoint ALREADY exposes per-view metadata (description, columns with type/mode/description/allowed_values/examples, example SQL queries). Source of truth is configs/datasets.yaml. No new backend work strictly required.
- MCP server (genetics-mcp-server): system prompt tells the LLM to call get_bigquery_schema which proxies the same /schema endpoint. Same data the LLM sees should be shown to users.

Cross-repo touchpoints:
- Main work: genetics-results-browser frontend (new UI for views + columns)
- Possibly: genetics-results-db (proxy /schema through the chat-app gateway if not already reachable, or enrich YAML)
- Possibly: genetics-mcp-server (only if we want to surface 'last tools used / which view this answer came from')

title: Add backend tests for share/fork endpoints
description: In tests/test_chat_history_router.py (or new test file) add tests: (1) share session - owner can share, non-owner gets 403. (2) get shared session - non-owner can access shared session, cannot access non-shared session (404). (3) fork session - authenticated user can fork a shared session, gets new session with copied messages. (4) unshare - after unsharing, non-owner gets 404.

title: Add share/unshare UI and read-only mode
description: In ChatPage.tsx header area: (1) If user is owner (isOwner=true): show 'Share' button that calls shareSession(id, true), copies URL to clipboard, shows snackbar confirmation. If already shared, show 'Unshare' button. (2) If user is NOT owner (isOwner=false, shared session): render chat in read-only mode (disable input, show info banner 'This is a shared chat by <owner>'), show 'Fork to continue' button that calls forkSession(), navigates to new session. (3) If getSession returns 404/error for a URL-based sessionId: show 'Chat not found or not shared with you' message.

title: Add URL routing for chat sessions
description: In App.tsx add route: /finngenie/chat/:sessionId -> ChatPage. In ChatPage.tsx: (1) read sessionId from useParams, (2) on mount, if sessionId is present, load that session via getSession (which now handles shared access), (3) use useNavigate to update URL when user selects/creates sessions (push /finngenie/chat/{id}), (4) when no session selected, URL is /finngenie. Keep existing / and /finngenie routes working as before.

title: Add frontend API functions for share/fork
description: In chatHistoryApi.ts add: (1) shareSession(sessionId, shared: boolean) - PUT to /chat/sessions/{id}/share. (2) forkSession(sessionId) - POST to /chat/sessions/{id}/fork, returns new session object. (3) Update getSession() to map new 'is_owner' and 'shared' fields. (4) Update SessionDetail type to include isOwner: boolean, shared: boolean.

title: Add share/fork backend API endpoints
description: In routers/chat_history.py add: (1) PUT /chat/sessions/{session_id}/share - auth_required, toggles shared flag (body: {shared: bool}), returns 403 if not owner. (2) POST /chat/sessions/{session_id}/fork - auth_required, forks session for current user, returns new session ID. (3) Modify GET /chat/sessions/{session_id} to use get_session_for_access - returns session if owned OR shared, include 'is_owner' and 'shared' fields in SessionDetailResponse. Return 404 if not accessible.

title: Add fork_session DB method
description: In chat_history_db.py add fork_session(source_session_id, target_user_id) that: (1) verifies source session is shared, (2) creates a new session owned by target_user_id with copied title (prefixed 'Fork of: '), (3) copies all chat_messages with new UUIDs but same content/role/order. Return the new ChatSession. Do NOT copy attachments for now (complexity).

title: Add DB methods for sharing and access control
description: In chat_history_db.py add: (1) set_shared(session_id, user_id, shared: bool) - sets shared flag, verifies user owns session. (2) get_session_for_access(session_id, user_id) - returns ChatSession + is_owner flag; returns session if user owns it OR if shared=1; raises/returns None otherwise. Update ChatSession dataclass to include 'shared' field.

title: Add shared column migration to chat_sessions table
description: Add 'shared' BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0 column to chat_sessions in _init_db() migration section of genetics-mcp-server/src/genetics_mcp_server/db/chat_history_db.py. Follow existing migration pattern (check if column exists, ALTER TABLE if not).

title: Feature: Chat Sharing with URL Routing
description: Add shareable chat URLs so each chat session has a unique URL (based on chat ID). By default, chats are private. Users can 'share this chat' to copy a shareable link. Unsharing should also be available. Show proper messages when accessing unshared chats. Consider allowing continuation of shared chats (requires DB work). Frontend is genetics-results-browser, backend is genetics-mcp-server.

title: Remove or restore PhenotypeChat export in phenotype/index.ts
description: src/features/phenotype/index.ts re-exports PhenotypeChat from a non-existent module. tsc --noEmit complains. Either restore the missing PhenotypeChat module/file or remove the export. Surfaced during validation of genetics-results-browser-1bg.3 — out of scope there, filed separately.

title: Update LLMChat height JSDoc default to 100dvh
description: src/features/chat/chat.types.ts line ~67 still documents the default as 'calc(100vh - 300px)'. The actual default in LLMChat.tsx is now 'calc(100dvh - 300px)' after genetics-results-browser-1bg.11. Trivial docstring sync.
